$comment: Gate spec for the v6 lineage — the FIRST spec cut from the SHIPPED model's own readings. Every floor is v264 (v6.3.0, the model in production) MINUS a per-metric noise margin, so the gate asks the question a promotion gate should ask: 'does this candidate regress against what we ship, beyond noise?'
$why_this_exists: v5.3.0-family was calibrated against the v2.2.0-era model and was never re-cut when the lineage moved to v6.x. By 2026-07-16 it had drifted into uselessness in BOTH directions: 16 of its 17 battery floors were slack by +1.8 to +50pp (a candidate could lose 27.9pp of us.street_suffix, or 48pp of us.intersection_real, and still PASS), while its one tight floor — fr.postcode 99.2 — FAILED THE SHIPPED MODEL (v264 reads 99.0). A bar that the thing in production cannot clear measures nothing. Verified by running the full battery on v264 itself: verdict FAIL, on fr.postcode, alone.
$margin_rationale: floor = v264's reading - max(1.0pp, 2 x the metric's 95% binomial half-width at its own support). NOT a uniform margin: the noise band varies 19x across these metrics (fr.postcode +-0.55pp at n=1288 near ceiling; fr.country +-10.4pp at n=86). A uniform 2pp would be 3.6x noise on fr.postcode — blessing real drift — and 0.2x noise on fr.country — failing on coin flips. 2 sigma means a candidate must be MEANINGFULLY worse than shipped to trip a floor. Where a scorer's support is not exposed, the margin falls back to 2.0pp, the project's own pre-publish regression bar; those are marked below.
$zero_margin_is_the_bug_do_not_repeat_it: v5.3.0's fr.postcode floor was set to EXACTLY what v2.2.0 read (99.2, $revision_2026_07_04), and that spec's own note predicted the consequence — 'any hair of postcode loss fails loudly'. It did. Cutting a fresh spec at floor == reading would recreate that defect: a zero-margin v6 cut fails v310 on 4 of 17 floors (us.postcode -0.2, us.region -0.2, arena.perturb -1.0, fr.postcode -0.1), every one inside noise. The margin is not generosity; it is the difference between a tripwire and a coin.
$this_spec_tightens_15_of_18: This is not a relaxation. 15 floors move UP, several by 10-48pp, because the shipped model is far better than the v5.3.0 bars on almost everything. Two move down: us.country_homograph_f1 (-0.2, noise) and fr.postcode (see its revision note). One is kept verbatim.
$revision_fr_postcode: LOOSENED 99.2 -> 97.9 (-1.3pp), and this is the one floor in this file that deserves an argument. THE DRIFT IS REAL AND UNEXPLAINED: v5.3.0 set 99.2 because v2.2.0 read 99.2; the SHIPPED v264 reads 99.0. Somewhere between those lineages FR postcode lost 0.2pp and either no one ran the gate or it was accepted without a spec update. Cutting from v264 BLESSES that drift permanently — a spec cut from current readings has no memory and can only ratchet down — so it is recorded here rather than absorbed silently. The new floor is v264's 99.0 minus 2x its 0.55pp noise band. It is TIGHTER than the project's own 2pp pre-publish rule would give (97.0). It is not tighter than 99.2, and it cannot be: 99.2 fails the model in production. SOMEONE SHOULD ESTABLISH whether the 99.2 -> 99.0 drift was adjudicated before this floor is treated as settled.
$revision_us_country_homograph: LOOSENED 83.3 -> 83.1 (-0.2pp). Falls out of the uniform 2.0pp fallback (v264 reads 85.1) because this scorer does not expose its support. Noise-level; no argument attached. If the support is ever exposed, re-derive.
$floor_fr_bare_street_intact: KEPT VERBATIM at 75.0 from v5.3.0 — NOT re-cut. This floor already carries a DESIGNED margin with a stated rationale (#949: the frozen 40-row OSM sample; 75% = 30/40 catches a real regression like v5.2.0's 40% while tolerating 1-2 rows of sample noise). Re-cutting a floor that was deliberately calibrated would throw away the calibration. Rule of thumb: this spec re-cuts REGRESSION BARS from the shipped model; it does not touch floors that encode a designed tolerance or a campaign TARGET. The v7 street 0.90 goal lives in the parity floors and must never be re-cut from a current reading — that would delete the objective.
label: v6.0.0-shipped-baseline
$provenance: Floors derived from /tmp/gate-v264 — the full battery run on the shipped v264 weights cache (package-shaped, #718-safe) at commit e08b337c on 2026-07-16. Reproduce: mailwoman eval gate --weights-cache <v264-cache> --gate v5.3.0-family.json. Per-tag supports from that run's fp32-per-locale.json.
